records.ety: [L. auspicium, fr. auspex: cf. F. auspice. See Auspicate, a.]

defs: A divining or taking of omens by observing birds; an omen as to an undertaking, drawn from birds; an augury; an omen or sign in general; an indication as to the future.

quotes: Which by his auspice they will nobler make.
notes: ☞ In this sense the word is generally plural, auspices; as, under the auspices of the king.
